name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism: Sudo and Sudo Caching
description: Privilege Escalation / TA0004. Run `sudo -n -l` (non-interactive list mode). Captures what the current user could escalate to via sudo without entering a password. Read-only — does NOT execute any sudo'd command. Useful for post-foothold privesc enumeration. On systems with no sudo entry for the current user, sudo exits 1; that exit code is captured in the envelope and is not a TTP failure.
exec: sudo -n -l 2>&1 || echo 'sudo unavailable or no entry (exit code captured in envelope)'
